INTERSECTION OF FISHTRAP ROAD AND BUTLER MILL ROAD HAS BEEN CLOSED DUE TO SIGNIFICANT WATER OVER THE ROAD. ADDITIONAL ROADS BETWEEN GRANT AND WOODVILLE HAVE BEEN CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

A NARROW PATH OF DAMAGE...MAINLY SNAPPED/UPROOTED TREES...OCCURRED JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 65 NEAR FRANCISCO IN NORTHWEST JACKSON COUNTY. THE DAMAGE EXTENDED ABOUT 1 MILE NORTHEAST INTO EXTREME SOUTHERN FRANKLIN COUNTY TENNESSEE...WHERE SEVERAL MORE TREES WERE SNAPPED JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 97. THE TORNADO WAS AT THE END OF ITS LIFE CYCLE AT THIS POINT AND LIFTED QUICKLY BEFORE REACHING THE HIGHER TERRAIN OF FRANKLIN COUNTY TENNESSEE. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS.

A LONG TRACK EF-3 TORNADO BEGAN IN EAST CENTRAL LIMESTONE COUNTY AND TRACKED OVER 34 MILES LIFTING IN EXTREME NORTHEAST MADISON COUNTY. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 250 YARDS. MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO 2-STORY HOMES WAS SEEN ALONG PEPPER ROAD BETWEEN MOORESVILLE ROAD AND EAST LIMESTONE ROAD. IN MADISON COUNTY...SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR HARVEST ALONG YARBROUGH ROAD. EF-3 DAMAGE WAS SEEN SOUTHEAST OF HAZEL GREEN WHERE SEVERAL HOMES WERE DESTOYED AND CONCRETE POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED.

AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF THE MADISON COUNTY EXECUTIVE AIRPORT IN MERIDIANVILLE AND TRACKED EAST-NORTHEAST FOR 7.3 MILES BEFORE LIFTING JUST EAST OF BUCKHORN HIGH SCHOOL. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG DARWIN ROAD AND MOORES MILL ROAD WHERE SEVERAL WOOD POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED AND SEVERAL METAL GRAIN SILOS WERE DESTROYED. OFF MORNING MIST DRIVE...A HOME WAS DESTROYED WITH NUMEROUS LARGE TREES SHEARED AND UPROOTED. INJURIES UNKNOWN BUT NO FATALITIES.

AN EF-0 TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN IN FARM FIELDS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF EASTER FARM ROAD AND CR 49/MORRIS ROAD. SEVERAL TREE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED IN THIS AREA ALONG WITH SPORADIC UPROOTING OF SOFTWOOD TREES. THE TORNADO THEN TRACKED EAST TOWARD THE ELKMONT COMMUNITY...MAINLY ALONG A PARALLEL TRACK JUST SOUTH OF MORRIS ROAD. THE TORNADO APPARENTLY LIFTED JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 127.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE WEST SIDE OF EDGEWOOD ROAD/CR 65 A LITTLE SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION WITH COFFMAN ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED IN THIS AREA WITH LARGE BRANCHES FROM HARDWOOD TREES ALSO SNAPPED. THE TORNADO MOVED IN AN EASTERLY DIRECTION CROSSING CR26 AND OLD ELKMONT ROAD. MINOR AND VERY SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG THE PATH. THE WIDTH NARROWED TO LESS THAN 50 YARDS AS IT MOVED FURTHER EAST AND FINALLY LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 31 AND I-65.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE WEST SIDE OF EDGEWOOD ROAD/CR 65 A LITTLE SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION WITH COFFMAN ROAD. MINOR AND VERY SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG THE PATH. THE WIDTH NARROWED TO LESS THAN 50 YARDS AS IT MOVED FURTHER EAST AND FINALLY LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 31 AND I-65.

AN EF1 TORNADO WITH PATH LENGTH OF 2.6 MILES AND MAX PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF THACH ROAD AND OAK GROVE ROAD. IT PARALLELED THACH ROAD BEFORE LIFTING EAST OF HIGHWAY 251. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED...SEVERAL HOMES HAD MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE INCLUDING LOSS OF ROOF SHINGLES AND SIDING DAMAGE. A WELL CONSTRUCTED BARN ALSO LOST MOST OF ITS ROOF.

UPDATE...FLOODING IS ONGOING NORTH OF MOULTON ON HWY 24. MOULTON TIRE IS SURROUNDED BY WATER AND THE PARKING LOT IS FLOODED. IN ADDITION...SEVERAL ROADS IN THE AREA HAVE BETWEEN 8 AND 15 INCHES OF WATER COVERING THEM. A FEW VEHICLES STALLED IN THE HIGH WATER AND WERE ABANDONED. NO INJURIES OR SWIFT WATER RESCUES WERE REPORTED AT THIS TIME.
